intro: Archaeocarex dregeana, also known as the dwarf club-rush or Drege's club-rush, is a perennial aquatic plant native to Africa, characterized by its dark green, strap-like leaves and small yellowish-brown flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Archaeocarex dregeana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory issues, fever, and digestive problems.
